definition: $250-350B in CLO reinvestment periods are expiring, creating an asymmetric response to steepening. Post-reinvestment CLOs cannot benefit from wider spreads and instead suffer rising defaults on legacy portfolios—a structural variable absent from standard macro frameworks.
evidence: pair_1 debate called this genuinely novel: 'a structural variable that doesn't appear in any standard macro framework'
This scale of simultaneous expiration is untested
Post-reinvestment CLOs have asymmetric exposure: cannot benefit from spread widening, only harmed by defaults
open_questions: How does simultaneous large-scale expiration interact with the CLO arbitrage viability threshold?
What is the precise timeline and magnitude of expirations?
Are there secondary market mechanisms that could absorb the asymmetric exposure?
